description.en: Mike Pitts's 2000 Century work — the principal accessible scholarly synthesis of the Late Neolithic and Early Bronze Age henge tradition in Britain, with Stonehenge as the central case. Pitts, longtime editor of *British Archaeology* and excavator of the Stonehenge environs, situates Stonehenge within the broader Wessex monumental tradition (Avebury, Silbury Hill, Durrington Walls, the Cursus monuments) and documents the substantial communal-ritual character of the construction across multiple phases c. 3000–1500 BCE.
